title: lappula duplicicarpa
intro: Lappula duplicicarpa, also known as the twin-fruited stickseed, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lappula duplicicarpa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Lappula duplicicarpa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its herbal uses, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
